segments.text: Lesson 5: Youth. First listen and then answer the following question.

translations.zh-Hans: 第5课：青年。先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: How does the writer like to treat young people?

translations.zh-Hans: 作者喜欢怎样对待年轻人？

segments.text: People are always talking about the problem of youth.

translations.zh-Hans: 人们总在谈论青年问题。

segments.text: If there is one, which I take leave to doubt, then it is older people who create it, not the young themselves.

translations.zh-Hans: 即便真有这样一个问题——对此我斗胆表示怀疑——那也是年长者造成的，并非年轻人自身。

segments.text: Let us get down to fundamentals and agree that the young are, after all, human beings—people just like their elders.

translations.zh-Hans: 让我们从根本说起，并承认年轻人终究也是人，是和长辈一样的人。

segments.text: There is only one difference between an old man and a young one.

translations.zh-Hans: 老人和年轻人之间只有一个区别。

segments.text: The young man has a glorious future before him, and the old one has a splendid future behind him, and maybe that is where the rub is.

translations.zh-Hans: 年轻人面前有辉煌的未来，老人身后则有灿烂的过去；也许症结就在这里。

segments.text: When I was a teenager, I felt that I was just young and uncertain, that I was a new boy in a huge school, and I would have been very pleased to be regarded as something so interesting as a problem.

translations.zh-Hans: 十几岁时，我觉得自己不过是年轻而缺乏自信，就像一所大学校里的新生；如果别人把我看成‘问题’这样有趣的事物，我会非常高兴。

segments.text: For one thing, being a problem gives you a certain identity.

translations.zh-Hans: 一方面，成为一个问题会赋予你某种身份。

segments.text: And that is one of the things the young are busily engaged in seeking.

translations.zh-Hans: 而身份正是年轻人正忙于寻找的东西之一。
我觉得年轻人令人振奋。

segments.text: They have an air of freedom, and they have not a dreary commitment to mean ambitions or love of comfort.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们身上有自由的气息，也没有乏味地执着于卑微的野心或贪图安逸。

segments.text: They are not anxious social climbers, and they have no devotion to material things.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们不急于向上钻营，也不迷恋物质。

segments.text: All this seems to me to link them with life and the origins of things.

translations.zh-Hans: 在我看来，这一切把他们同生命及万物的本源联系起来。

segments.text: It is as if they were, in some sense, cosmic beings in violent and lovely contrast with us suburban creatures.

translations.zh-Hans: 从某种意义上说，他们仿佛是宇宙中的生灵，与我们这些郊区俗人形成强烈而美好的对照。

segments.text: All that is in my mind when I meet a young person.

translations.zh-Hans: 每当遇见年轻人，我心中想到的就是这些。

segments.text: He may be conceited, ill-mannered, presumptuous or fatuous, but I do not turn for protection to dreary clichés about respect for elders.

translations.zh-Hans: 他也许自负、无礼、冒失或愚蠢，但我不会躲到‘尊敬长辈’之类乏味的陈词滥调后面寻求庇护。

segments.text: As if mere age were a reason for respect.

translations.zh-Hans: 仿佛年龄本身就是值得尊敬的理由。

segments.text: I accept that we are equals, and I will argue with him as an equal if I think he is wrong.

translations.zh-Hans: 我承认我们彼此平等；如果我认为他错了，就会以平等身份同他争论。
